structure_label: Verb sequence with « faire + infinitif » causative
core_meaning: Expresses having someone else do something (causative construction)
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing causation—making or having someone do something
when_to_use: When describing requests or orders to others to perform actions
key_variations: Je fais réparer ma voiture (I have my car repaired).
Elle a fait construire une maison (She had a house built).
Nous avons fait manger les enfants (We made the children eat).
essential_vocabulary_slots: faire, réparer, construire, manger, laisser, compléter
common_learner_mistakes: Incorrect pronoun placement with direct object
chunk_compatibility: Causative structures support high-frequency requests and commands
primary_function: Expressing causation—making, having, or getting someone to do something
key_forms: Je fais faire, tu fais faire, il/elle fait faire, nous faisons faire, vous faites faire, ils/elles font faire
essential_vocabulary: faire, réparer, construire, manger, partir, venir, travailler, apprendre
why_it_matters: Causative constructions enable expressing complex social interactions and instructions

core_meaning: Expresses permission, allowance, or causation (let/leave/have something done)
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing permission or allowance for actions
when_to_use: When describing passive allowance or permission for someone to do something
key_variations: Je laisse entrer les enfants (I let the children in).
Elle laisse tomber ses clés (She lets/drops her keys).
Ils laissent travailler leurs employés (They let their employees work).
essential_vocabulary_slots: laisser, entrer, tomber, travailler, parler, faire, partir
chunk_compatibility: Permission structures for allowing and enabling actions
key_forms: Je laisse, tu laisses, il/elle laisse, nous laissons, vous laissez, ils/elles laissent
essential_vocabulary: laisser, entrer, sortir, parler, faire, tomber, partir, rester
why_it_matters: Laisser + infinitif conveys permission with less direct control than faire

core_meaning: Expresses perceiving an action happening (see someone do something)
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing perception of ongoing actions or events
when_to_use: When describing what someone witnesses or observes happening
key_variations: Je l'ai vu partir (I saw him leave).
Nous avons vu voler les oiseaux (We saw the birds flying).
Elle a vu casser la vitre (She saw the window break).
essential_vocabulary_slots: voir, partir, voler, casser, arriver, entrer, tomber
common_learner_mistakes: Using 'de' after voir instead of direct infinitif
Incorrect agreement of past participle with perceived object
chunk_compatibility: Perception structures for describing witnessed events
primary_function: Expressing perception of actions and events
key_forms: Je vois, tu vois, il/elle voit, nous voyons, vous voyez, ils/elles voient
essential_vocabulary: voir, partir, arriver, entrer, tomber, sauter, voler, courir
why_it_matters: Voir + infinitif allows expressing perception without needing 'happening' verbs

core_meaning: Expresses perceiving a sound or action (hear someone do something)
when_to_use: When describing what someone hears happening, including voices and sounds
key_variations: J'ai entendu chanter dans la rue (I heard singing in the street).
Nous l'avons entendu crier (We heard him shout).
Elle a entendu ouvrir la porte (She heard the door open).
essential_vocabulary_slots: entendre, chanter, crier, ouvrir, parler, frapper, appeler
common_learner_mistakes: Using past participle instead of infinitif
chunk_compatibility: Auditory perception structures for describing sounds and heard events
primary_function: Expressing auditory perception of actions and events
key_forms: J'entends, tu entends, il/elle entend, nous entendons, vous entendez, ils/elles entendent
essential_vocabulary: entendre, chanter, crier, parler, frapper, appeler, casser, tomber
why_it_matters: Entendre + infinitif conveys passive hearing without intentional listening

core_meaning: Expresses sending someone to do something
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing delegation of tasks through sending
when_to_use: When describing sending someone to accomplish a specific task or goal
key_variations: Je l'ai envoyé chercher du pain (I sent him to get bread).
Elle envoie ses enfants étudier (She sends her children to study).
Ils envoient un représentant négocier (They send a representative to negotiate).
essential_vocabulary_slots: envoyer, chercher, étudier, négocier, travailler, apprendre, faire
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with other verb + infinitif constructions
Misplacing pronouns between envoyer and infinitif
chunk_compatibility: Task delegation structures for expressing assignments
primary_function: Expressing sending or dispatching someone to perform an action
key_forms: J'envoie, tu envoies, il/elle envoie, nous envoyons, vous envoyez, ils/elles envoient
essential_vocabulary: envoyer, chercher, étudier, travailler, apprendre, faire, négocier, vérifier
why_it_matters: Envoyer + infinitif enables expressing task delegation and assignments

core_meaning: Expresses what appears to happen or seem
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing appearance and inference about actions
when_to_use: When describing what seems to be happening or appears to be true
pattern: Sujet + sembler + infinitif / Il semble que + subjonctif
key_variations: Il semble avoir compris (He seems to have understood).
Elle semble être partie (She seems to have left).
Cela semble être correct (That seems to be correct).
essential_vocabulary_slots: sembler, avoir, être, comprendre, partir, rester, arriver
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with impersonal constructions (Il semble que)
chunk_compatibility: Appearance and inference structures for expressing uncertainty
key_forms: Je semble, il/elle semble, nous semblons, vous semblez, ils/elles semblent
essential_vocabulary: sembler, avoir, être, comprendre, partir, savoir, connaître, vouloir
why_it_matters: Sembler + infinitif allows expressing inference without certainty

core_meaning: Expresses intention to do something (count on doing)
when_to_use: When describing plans or intentions for the near future
key_variations: Je compte partir demain (I plan to leave tomorrow).
Nous comptons vous revoir bientôt (We plan to see you again soon).
Elle compte changer de travail (She intends to change jobs).
essential_vocabulary_slots: compter, partir, revoir, changer, rester, arriver, revenir
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with 'compter sur' (to count on/rely on)
Using compter instead of avoir l'intention de
chunk_compatibility: Future intention structures for expressing plans
primary_function: Expressing plans, intentions, and near-future actions
key_forms: Je compte, tu comptes, il/elle compte, nous comptons, vous comptez, ils/elles comptent
essential_vocabulary: compter, partir, arriver, rester, revenir, changer, acheter, voyager
why_it_matters: Compter + infinitif expresses certain future intentions more directly than aller

structure_label: Verb sequence with « aller + infinitif » (near future)
core_meaning: Expresses near future action (going to do something)
when_to_use: When describing actions that will happen soon or in the immediate future
key_variations: Je vais manger (I'm going to eat).
Nous allons partir (We're going to leave).
Ils vont construire une maison (They're going to build a house).
essential_vocabulary_slots: aller, manger, partir, construire, arriver, voir, faire
chunk_compatibility: Near-future structures for immediate actions and plans
primary_function: Expressing immediate or near-future intentions and actions
key_forms: Je vais, tu vas, il/elle va, nous allons, vous allez, ils/elles vont
essential_vocabulary: aller, manger, partir, arriver, voir, faire, savoir, comprendre
why_it_matters: Aller + infinitif is the most common way to express immediate future in French

structure_label: Verb sequence with « venir de + infinitif » (recent past)
core_meaning: Expresses that something has just happened
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing recent or immediately past actions
when_to_use: When describing actions that just happened moments ago
key_variations: Je viens de finir (I just finished).
Elle vient de partir (She just left).
Nous venons de manger (We just ate).
essential_vocabulary_slots: venir, finir, partir, manger, arriver, voir, apprendre
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with venir à (to happen to)
Using past tense instead of present for recent past
Placing infinitif directly after venir instead of de
chunk_compatibility: Recent past structures for expressing just-completed actions
primary_function: Expressing recent or immediately past actions
key_forms: Je viens de, tu viens de, il/elle vient de, nous venons de, vous venez de, ils/elles viennent de
essential_vocabulary: venir, finir, partir, arriver, manger, apprendre, découvrir, entendre
why_it_matters: Venir de + infinitif expresses recent past without using past tense

core_meaning: Expresses obligation, necessity, or strong probability
when_to_use: When describing what must or should be done, or what is likely true
key_variations: Je dois partir (I must leave).
Il doit être malade (He must be sick).
Nous devons travailler plus (We must work more).
essential_vocabulary_slots: devoir, partir, être, travailler, faire, arriver, comprendre
common_learner_mistakes: Using devoir for requests instead of imperatives
chunk_compatibility: Obligation and necessity structures for expressing requirements
primary_function: Expressing obligation, necessity, and logical probability
key_forms: Je dois, tu dois, il/elle doit, nous devons, vous devez, ils/elles doivent
essential_vocabulary: devoir, faire, partir, travailler, étudier, arriver, être, savoir
why_it_matters: Devoir + infinitif is essential for expressing obligations and inferences

when_to_use: When describing what is possible, allowed, or within one's ability
key_variations: Je peux venir (I can come).
Elle peut avoir raison (She may be right).
Nous pouvons partir maintenant (We can leave now).
essential_vocabulary_slots: pouvoir, venir, avoir, partir, faire, aider, rester
common_learner_mistakes: Using pouvoir for requests instead of permission contexts
chunk_compatibility: Possibility and permission structures for expressing capability
primary_function: Expressing possibility, permission, ability, and potential actions
key_forms: Je peux, tu peux, il/elle peut, nous pouvons, vous pouvez, ils/elles peuvent
essential_vocabulary: pouvoir, venir, faire, aider, rester, aller, voir, parler
why_it_matters: Pouvoir + infinitif expresses possibility and permission across contexts

core_meaning: Expresses knowing how to do something (skill/ability)
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing acquired skills and learned abilities
when_to_use: When describing skills or learned abilities that someone possesses
key_variations: Je sais nager (I know how to swim).
Elle sait cuisiner (She knows how to cook).
Ils savent parler trois langues (They know how to speak three languages).
essential_vocabulary_slots: savoir, nager, cuisiner, parler, danser, conduire, chanter
common_learner_mistakes: Using savoir for single actions instead of pouvoir
Treating savoir and pouvoir as completely interchangeable
chunk_compatibility: Skill expression structures for describing learned abilities
primary_function: Expressing skills, knowledge, and learned abilities
key_forms: Je sais, tu sais, il/elle sait, nous savons, vous savez, ils/elles savent
essential_vocabulary: savoir, nager, cuisiner, parler, danser, conduire, chanter, jouer
why_it_matters: Savoir + infinitif distinguishes acquired skills from general possibility

when_to_use: When describing what someone wants, wishes, or desires to do
key_variations: Je veux apprendre (I want to learn).
Elle veut voyager (She wants to travel).
Nous voulons réussir (We want to succeed).
essential_vocabulary_slots: vouloir, apprendre, voyager, réussir, rester, partir, aider
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing vouloir with désirer or souhaiter
chunk_compatibility: Desire and intention structures for expressing wants
primary_function: Expressing desires, wishes, and intentional actions
key_forms: Je veux, tu veux, il/elle veut, nous voulons, vous voulez, ils/elles veulent
essential_vocabulary: vouloir, apprendre, voyager, réussir, aider, rester, partir, faire
why_it_matters: Vouloir + infinitif is fundamental for expressing desires and preferences

when_to_use: When describing what someone prefers to do compared to alternatives
key_variations: Je préfère attendre (I prefer to wait).
Elle préfère partir tôt (She prefers to leave early).
Nous préférons rester ici (We prefer to stay here).
essential_vocabulary_slots: préférer, attendre, partir, rester, faire, aller, manger
common_learner_mistakes: Not using 'plutôt que' for explicit alternatives
chunk_compatibility: Preference structures for comparing action choices
primary_function: Expressing preferences and choice between actions
key_forms: Je préfère, tu préfères, il/elle préfère, nous préférons, vous préférez, ils/elles préfèrent
essential_vocabulary: préférer, attendre, partir, rester, faire, manger, boire, aller
why_it_matters: Préférer + infinitif enables expressing nuanced preferences

structure_label: Verb sequence with « essayer de + infinitif »
core_meaning: Expresses attempting or trying to do something
primary_communicative_fn: Expressing attempts and efforts to accomplish actions
when_to_use: When describing trying, attempting, or making efforts to do something
key_variations: J'essaie de comprendre (I'm trying to understand).
Elle essaie de maigrir (She's trying to lose weight).
Nous essayons de trouver une solution (We're trying to find a solution).
essential_vocabulary_slots: essayer, comprendre, maigrir, trouver, aider, faire, apprendre
common_learner_mistakes: Confusing with 'essayer' without preposition in other contexts (essayer des vêtements)
Using essayer as a simple verb instead of a modal
chunk_compatibility: Effort and attempt structures for expressing trying actions
primary_function: Expressing attempts, efforts, and trying to accomplish actions
key_forms: J'essaie de, tu essaies de, il/elle essaie de, nous essayons de, vous essayez de, ils/elles essaient de
essential_vocabulary: essayer, comprendre, maigrir, trouver, faire, aider, apprendre, parler
why_it_matters: Essayer de + infinitif expresses deliberate attempts and efforts
